events.title: EU AI Act enters into force
events.description: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 enters into force 20 days after publication in the Official Journal. Most provisions apply on a staggered timeline over the following 36 months.

events.title: Prohibited practices apply
events.description: Prohibitions on banned AI practices (Article 5) and AI literacy requirements (Article 4) become enforceable. Systems performing prohibited practices must cease operation in the EU.

events.title: GPAI rules apply
events.description: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models take effect, including documentation, copyright compliance, training data summaries, and additional obligations for models with systemic risk.

events.title: High-risk requirements apply
events.description: Full requirements for high-risk AI systems (Articles 6-15, 26-27) and transparency obligations (Article 50) become enforceable. Providers must meet risk management, data governance, documentation, record-keeping, transparency, human oversight, accuracy, and robustness requirements. Registration in the EU database and conformity assessment required before market placement.

events.title: Legacy GPAI and large-scale IT systems
events.description: Obligations apply to GPAI models already on the market before August 2025, and to high-risk AI systems that are components of large-scale EU IT systems (e.g., Schengen Information System, Visa Information System, Eurodac) listed in Annex X.

events.title: Product safety components
events.description: Obligations for high-risk AI systems that are safety components of products covered by Annex I Union harmonisation legislation (Pathway A under Article 6(1)) take full effect. These systems must comply with all high-risk requirements and undergo relevant conformity assessment procedures.
